debug_info.goal:
U V : Type
f : U → V
⊢ onto f ↔ range f = Univ V

failed_attempts:
by 
  unfold onto
  unfold range
  unfold Univ
  ext v
  simp
  <;> tauto

by
  constructor
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = V
    intro h
    ext v
    simp [Set.mem_range]
    have h1 : ∃ u : U, f u = v := h v (by simp)
    rcases h1 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu⟩
  · -- Assume range f = V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v hv
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
      have h2 : range f = (Set.univ : Set V) := by
        exact_mod_cast h
      rw [h2] at *
      simp at *
    rcases h1 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu⟩

by 
  simp [Set.ext_iff, Function.Onto]
  <;> aesop

by
  constructor
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = Univ V
    intro h
    ext v
    simp [h]
    <;> tauto
  · -- Assume range f = Univ V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
      rw [h]
      simp
    rcases h1 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, by simpa using hu⟩

by
  apply Iff.intro
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = Unv V
    intro h
    ext v
    simp [Set.mem_range, Set.mem_univ]
    exact h v
  · -- Assume range f = Unv V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
      rw [h]
      simp
    rcases h1 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu⟩

by
  constructor
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = Univ V
    intro h
    have h1 : ∀ v : V, ∃ u : U, f u = v := by
      intro v
      exact h v
    ext v
    simp at *
    specialize h1 v
    simpa using h1
  · -- Assume range f = Univ V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
      rw [show range f = Univ V by exact h]
      simp
    rcases h1 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu⟩

by
  apply Iff.intro
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = V
    intro h
    have h1 : ∀ v : V, v ∈ range f := by
      intro v
      have h2 : v ∈ range f := by
        simpa using h v
      exact h2
    have h2 : range f = Set.univ := by
      ext v
      simp at *
      all_goals
        try { 
          tauto 
        }
        try { 
          exact h1 v 
        }
    exact_mod_cast h2
  · -- Assume range f = V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v
    have h2 : v ∈ range f := by 
      rw [h]
      simp
    rcases h2 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu⟩

by
  apply Iff.intro
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = V
    intro h
    ext v
    simp [mem_range]
    exact h v
  · -- Assume range f = V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by 
      rw [show range f = Univ V by exact h]
      simp
    simp [mem_range] at h1
    exact h1

by
  constructor
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = Univ V
    intro h
    ext v
    simp at *
    <;> try { 
      have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
        have h2 : ∃ u, f u = v := h v
        rcases h2 with ⟨u, hu⟩
        exact ⟨u, hu⟩
      tauto
    }
  · -- Assume range f = Univ V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
      rw [show range f = Univ V by simpa using h]
      simp
    rcases h1 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu⟩

by
  constructor
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = univ V
    intro h
    ext v
    simp at *
    -- We need to show that v ∈ range f
    -- This is equivalent to ∃ u : U, f u = v
    -- This is the definition of f being onto
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
      simpa using h v
    tauto
  · -- Assume range f = univ V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v
    -- We need to show ∃ u : U, f u = v
    -- Since range f = univ V, we have v ∈ range f
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
      rw [show range f = univ V by exact_mod_cast h]
      simp
    -- v ∈ range f means ∃ u : U, f u = v
    rcases h1 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu⟩

by
  constructor
  · -- Assume f is onto, prove range f = V
    intro h
    ext v
    simp at *
    <;> try { 
      constructor 
      · -- Assume v ∈ range f, prove v ∈ V
        rintro ⟨u, hu⟩
        tauto
      · -- Assume v ∈ V, prove v ∈ range f
        intro h
        have h2 : v ∈ range f := by
          have h3 : ∃ u : U, f u = v := h v
          simpa using h3
        tauto
    }
  · -- Assume range f = V, prove f is onto
    intro h
    intro v hv
    have h1 : v ∈ range f := by
      rw [show range f = Univ V by exact_mod_cast h]
      simp at *
    rcases h1 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    exact ⟨u, hu⟩
